content.text: Data Steward Roles and Accountability Model

Deploying Claude across an enterprise creates data governance obligations that cannot be owned by a single team. Who decides which data is safe to include in a RAG corpus? Who approves a new Claude use case that processes HR records? Who is accountable when a data residency violation is discovered? Without a clear stewardship model, these questions generate delays, inconsistent decisions, and compliance gaps.

attrs.alt: Data stewardship accountability model diagram for Claude AI programs

content.text: The accountability model above defines five layers: the CDO holds board-level accountability; the DPO, AI Governance Lead, and CISO share programme-level ownership; domain data stewards own the data assets within their business units; and technical stewards embedded in app teams translate policy into code.
The CDO is the executive accountable for data strategy, governance frameworks, and the organisation's data risk posture. For Claude programmes specifically:
Approves the enterprise AI data governance policy and its revisions.
Chairs the AI Data Governance Committee (or delegates to AI Governance Lead).
Provides board-level reporting on AI data risk and incident metrics.
Signs off on high-risk Claude use cases (e.g. processing sensitive personal data at scale).
The DPO is the GDPR-designated role responsible for ensuring Claude deployments comply with data protection law. DPO responsibilities in a Claude context:
Reviews and approves Data Processing Agreements with Anthropic.
Maintains the Record of Processing Activities (RoPA) entry for each Claude application.
Signs Transfer Impact Assessments for EU→US API routing.
Responds to data subject access requests that implicate AI-generated outputs.
Monitors regulatory guidance on AI and data protection (EU AI Act, EDPB opinions).
In organisations without a mandatory DPO, these responsibilities fall to the Chief Privacy Officer or General Counsel's privacy team.
The AI Governance Lead is the operational hub of the stewardship model. This role owns the day-to-day governance process rather than executive accountability or legal compliance:
Authors and maintains the enterprise AI data governance policy and use-case register.
Runs the intake process for new Claude use cases, coordinating DPO and domain steward sign-off.
Maintains the AI risk register: tracks identified risks, mitigations, and residual risk for each deployment.
Coordinates cross-domain stewards when a Claude application spans multiple business units.
Owns the governance tooling: the use-case register, policy portal, and compliance dashboard.

Domain data stewards are accountable for specific data assets within a business unit. They are not AI specialists — they are the subject-matter experts who know what their data means, who may access it, and what the consequences of misuse are.
Employee records, performance reviews, org charts
Approve which HR data enters Claude's RAG corpus; set ACLs; review deletion requests
Classify financial data tiers; approve use cases that output financial analysis; retain lineage records
Approve which legal documents can be retrieved by Claude; review privileged-material risks
Customer data, product telemetry, source code
Approve customer data use in Claude; review prompt injection risks in code-generation apps
Support tickets, customer PII, interaction logs
Approve support-assistant use cases; set retention windows; review DSAR impacts
Technical stewards are engineers or architects embedded in Claude application teams. Their role is to implement governance policy in code and surface violations back to the governance layer:
Implement data classification checks in the request pipeline.
Maintain lineage logging and ensure schema compliance with the enterprise standard.
Run automated policy validation in CI/CD (schema tests, ACL checks, residency assertions).
Report monthly metrics to the AI Governance Lead: request volumes, classification distributions, violation counts.
Escalate anomalies — unexpected data classifications, spikes in residency violations, unusual prompt patterns — within 24 hours.
The RACI matrix below assigns Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed roles for the key governance activities in a Claude programme.
Classify data assets for Claude RAG
Respond to data subject access request

Every new Claude application or significant change to an existing one must pass through the intake process before processing real data. The AI Governance Lead runs the process; domain stewards and the DPO provide input.

# claude-use-case-intake.yaml
# Complete one per new Claude application or major feature change.

use_case_id: uc-2025-042
title: "HR Policy Assistant"
owner_team: "People Operations Engineering"
ai_governance_lead_reviewer: "Jane Smith"

data_assets:
  - source: "Workday HR records"
    domain_steward: "HR Data Steward (Alice Brown)"
    classification: "confidential"
    includes_personal_data: true
    gdpr_lawful_basis: "legitimate_interest"
  - source: "Employee handbook PDFs"
    domain_steward: "HR Data Steward"
    classification: "internal"
    includes_personal_data: false

residency:
  user_jurisdiction: "EU"
  preferred_claude_endpoint: "eu-west-1"
  transfer_mechanism: "eu_endpoint_no_transfer"
  tia_required: false

risk_assessment:
  identified_risks:
    - "Employees may ask Claude about their own performance reviews — risk of hallucination"
    - "HR policy may be mis-cited with incorrect effective dates"
  mitigations:
    - "RAG corpus limited to approved HR policy documents only — no individual records"
    - "All responses include disclaimer and link to authoritative HR portal"
  residual_risk: "low"

approvals:
  domain_steward_sign_off: pending
  dpo_sign_off: pending
  ai_governance_lead_sign_off: pending
  approval_date: null
Technical steward implements per approved spec; registers in use-case register
Approved but specific mitigations required before go-live
Technical steward implements conditions; AI Gov Lead re-reviews before launch
Additional information needed (TIA not complete, domain steward unavailable)
AI Gov Lead tracks; use case cannot proceed until resolved
Unacceptable risk, no viable mitigation, or outside policy scope
Requester notified with written rationale; escalation path available to CDO
Technical stewards submit a monthly governance report to the AI Governance Lead. The report is machine-generated from lineage logs and violation records.

import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

def generate_monthly_report(
    app_id: str,
    month: str,  # "2025-06"
    lineage_log: str = "logs/claude_lineage.jsonl",
    violation_log: str = "logs/cross_border_transfers.jsonl",
) -> dict:
    spans = [json.loads(l) for l in Path(lineage_log).read_text().splitlines() if l]
    month_spans = [s for s in spans if s.get("start_time", "").startswith(month)]

    api_calls = [s for s in month_spans if s.get("name") == "claude.api_call"]
    violations = []
    if Path(violation_log).exists():
        violations = [
            json.loads(l) for l in Path(violation_log).read_text().splitlines()
            if l and json.loads(l).get("timestamp", "").startswith(month)
               and json.loads(l).get("event") == "TRANSFER_BLOCKED"
        ]

    classifications = {}
    for s in month_spans:
        cls = s.get("attributes", {}).get("claude.data_classification", "unknown")
        classifications[cls] = classifications.get(cls, 0) + 1

    return {
        "report_type": "monthly_governance",
        "app_id": app_id,
        "month": month,
        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "metrics": {
            "total_spans": len(month_spans),
            "api_calls": len(api_calls),
            "residency_violations": len(violations),
            "classification_distribution": classifications,
        },
        "action_items": (
            [f"{len(violations)} residency violations require DPO review"]
            if violations else []
        ),
    }

report = generate_monthly_report("hr-policy-assistant", "2025-06")
print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))

A stewardship model only works if each role holder understands their responsibilities and can carry them out. Onboarding new stewards requires structured training, documented authority, and a clear escalation path.
Onboarding Checklist for New Domain Stewards

# domain-steward-onboarding.yaml

role: "Domain Data Steward — Finance"
steward_name: ""
start_date: ""

week_1:
  - "Read enterprise AI data governance policy (latest version)"
  - "Complete GDPR foundations training (≥ 2h, certificate required)"
  - "Meet with AI Governance Lead: review use-case register for Finance domain"
  - "Review all active Claude applications that consume Finance data"
  - "Obtain read access to lineage dashboard"

week_2:
  - "Shadow outgoing steward / AI Governance Lead on one intake review"
  - "Attend monthly governance committee meeting as observer"
  - "Review pending data classification decisions for Finance data assets"
  - "Complete Claude enterprise deployment training module"

week_3_4:
  - "Take primary sign-off for one low-risk use-case intake (supervised)"
  - "Complete first monthly data asset audit for Finance domain"
  - "Register in the Steward Directory with contact details and backup steward"

ongoing:
  - "Monthly: review lineage reports from technical stewards"
  - "Quarterly: audit data asset classifications against actual usage"
  - "Annually: review and re-approve all active Claude use cases in domain"
  - "On-change: review any new Claude use case or major change within 5 business days"

Disagreements between stewards are inevitable — a domain steward may want to include data in a Claude corpus that the DPO considers too sensitive, or a technical steward may flag a violation that the domain steward believes is a false positive. The governance model must specify how these disputes are resolved.
the two disagreeing parties attempt resolution within 3 business days. Technical steward escalates to domain steward; domain steward escalates to AI Governance Lead.
if bilateral resolution fails, the AI Governance Lead reviews the evidence and issues a binding decision within 5 business days.
if the dispute involves legal compliance risk (GDPR, AI Act) or significant business impact, it escalates to the CDO and DPO jointly. Decision required within 10 business days.

any steward may invoke an emergency stop on a Claude application if they have reasonable grounds to believe a data protection violation is ongoing. The AI Governance Lead must convene a review within 24 hours.

# emergency_stop.py — callable by any steward with appropriate credentials

import json
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path

STOP_REGISTER = Path("config/emergency_stops.json")

def invoke_emergency_stop(
    app_id: str,
    invoking_steward: str,
    reason: str,
    evidence_ref: str,  # link to log file or ticket
) -> dict:
    stops = json.loads(STOP_REGISTER.read_text()) if STOP_REGISTER.exists() else {}
    record = {
        "status": "ACTIVE",
        "invoked_by": invoking_steward,
        "invoked_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "reason": reason,
        "evidence_ref": evidence_ref,
        "reviewed_by": None,
        "resolved_at": None,
    }
    stops[app_id] = record
    STOP_REGISTER.write_text(json.dumps(stops, indent=2))

    # In production: also set a feature flag / circuit breaker
    # and send an alert to the AI Governance Lead and CDO
    print(f"EMERGENCY STOP invoked for {app_id} by {invoking_steward}: {reason}")
    return record

def check_emergency_stop(app_id: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if this app has an active emergency stop."""
    if not STOP_REGISTER.exists():
        return False
    stops = json.loads(STOP_REGISTER.read_text())
    return stops.get(app_id, {}).get("status") == "ACTIVE"

A data stewardship model for Claude programmes needs five roles with distinct, non-overlapping accountabilities: the CDO holds board-level responsibility; the DPO owns legal compliance; the AI Governance Lead manages the day-to-day governance process and risk register; domain data stewards own specific data assets and decide what enters Claude's context; technical stewards implement governance in code and report metrics monthly. The RACI matrix formalises who is Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, and Informed for each governance activity, eliminating ambiguity when decisions need to be made quickly. Every new Claude use case passes through a documented intake process with domain steward and DPO sign-off before touching real data.
